frontier.hypothesis: PERM_LINESWAP over the inline block's 6-8 non-CALL statements discovers a schedule-space arrangement outside the s3 hand-swept 13 structural axes and s4-s5 wrap/carrier probes.
frontier.mechanism: PERM_LINESWAP enumerates all dataflow-valid orderings of the specified line block, complementing directed-PERM_GENERAL (s14) with systematic O(n!)-minus-invalid coverage.
frontier.next_probe: Permuter modality: add PERM_LINESWAP around the inline block's non-CALL statements in tmp/perm_csmd4/base.c; launch fresh h5-basin campaign with LINESWAP as the only PERM directive; each ordering re-verified against engine sandbox (workspace UNFAITHFUL caveat per WIP notes) — sub-baseline finds must be splice-verified before promotion. Audit the alternative set BEFORE launch to guarantee no g3-basin spellings sneak in (s14 lesson: mixed base_score dropped to 40 and redid g3 exploration).

frontier.hypothesis: External Kengo (nm_cpu/cpu_side_move_dir_4) source glance surfaces the original C shape and points to a distinct structural axis not covered by m2c or marionation_Exec sibling.
frontier.mechanism: The src/system.c:497 tombstone flags `kengo:HIGH  |  nm_cpu/cpu_side_move_dir_4  |  160i  |  x4 size collision` — Kengo carries a same-name analog with a 4x-size difference indicating a superset. Original Lightweight source likely factored csmd4 as a subroutine of the Kengo function.
frontier.next_probe: Operator supplies a Kengo source dump (or an inspection command against an available Kengo binary/scratch) so a rederive-modality session can diff Kengo's `nm_cpu/cpu_side_move_dir_4` C body against csmd4's target asm. Absent operator supply, this frontier remains unavailable (WIP notes: `no known second twin in the codebase per notes.md`).
